pokemon:parasect.name: Parasect
pokemon:parasect.summary: Parasect is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Parasect is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.
A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.
A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.
When nothing's left to extract from the bug, the mushrooms on its back leave spores on the bug's egg.
A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls PARASECT. It scatters poisonous spores.
PARASECT are known to infest the roots of large trees en masse and drain nutrients. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
It scatters toxic spores from the mushroom cap. In China, the spores are used as herbal medicine.
It stays mostly in dark, damp places, the preference not of the bug, but of the big mush­ rooms on its back.
It stays mostly in dark, damp places, the preference not of the bug, but of the big mushroom on its back.
A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.
Mushroom-lacking specimens of this Pokémon lie unmoving in the forest, lending credence to the hypothesis that the large mushroom is in control of Parasect's actions.
The bug host is drained of energy by the mushroom on its back. The mushroom appears to do all the thinking.
The bug host is drained of energy by the mushroom on its back. The mushroom appears to do all the thinking.
It scatters toxic spores from its mushroom cap. Once harvested, these spores can be steeped and boiled down to prepare herbal medicines.
Parasect is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
It is controlled by a mushroom grown larger than the bug body. It is said to prefer damp places.
A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls PARASECT. It scatters poisonous spores.
A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.
PARASECT is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
PARASECT is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mush­ room spores it scatters.
The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.
The large mushroom on its back controls it. It often fights over territory with Shiinotic.
Its poisonous spores are also used in traditional medicine. Apparently, spores produced in Alola are not of very good quality.
The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.
A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.
A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.
A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.
The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.
The bug host is drained of energy by the mushrooms on its back. They appear to do all the thinking.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Parasect is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.

pokemon:paras.name: Paras
pokemon:paras.summary: Paras has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from this Bug Pokémon host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Paras has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from this Bug Pokémon host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.
Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.
Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by draw­ ing nutrients from the bug host.
The tochukaso growing on this POKéMON's back orders it to extract juice from tree trunks.
Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host PARAS.
A PARAS has parasitic tochukaso mushrooms growing on its back. They grow by drawing nutrients from the host. They are valued as a medicine for long life.
Growing out of the bug’s back are mushrooms called tochukaso. The mushrooms grow with the bug host.
It is doused with mushroom spores when it is born. As its body grows, mushrooms sprout from its back.
It is doused with mushroom spores when it is born. As its body grows, mushrooms sprout from its back.
Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by drawing nutrients from the bug host.
Sometimes seen at the foot of trees in humid forests. The mushrooms on its back—called tochukaso—are not present on infant specimens and instead emerge as Paras matures.
Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.
Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.
Mushrooms called tochukaso sprout from its back. They can be dried and powdered to make a medicine used to extend life.
Paras has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from this Bug Pokémon host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host PARAS.
Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host PARAS.
Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by draw­ ing nutrients from the bug host.
PARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from the BUG POKéMON host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
PARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from this BUG POKéMON host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
As its body grows large, oriental mushrooms named tochukaso start sprouting out of its back.
As its body grows, large mushrooms named tochukaso start sprouting out of its back.
No matter how much it eats, the mushrooms growing on its back steal away most of the nutrients it consumes.
The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.
Whether it’s due to a lack of moisture or a lack of nutrients, in Alola the mushrooms on Paras don’t grow up quite right.
Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.
Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.
Growing out of the bug’s back are mushrooms called tochukaso. The mushrooms grow with the bug host.
Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.
Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Paras has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from this Bug Pokémon host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
